cards.back: A mechanism that allows a child class to inherit attributes and methods from a parent class. Enables code reuse and creates an 'is-a' relationship.
A relationship where one class is a specialized version of another. Example: Dog is-a Animal. Dog inherits from Animal and adds dog-specific behavior.
Gives access to parent class methods. Use super().__init__() to call the parent constructor, or super().method() to call parent methods.
Redefining a parent method in a child class with the same name but different implementation. Allows child classes to customize behavior.
The ability of objects of different classes to respond to the same method call in different ways. Example: Circle.area() and Square.area() use different formulas.
A class that cannot be instantiated directly. Serves as a template for subclasses. Defined using ABC module with @abstractmethod decorators.
A decorator that marks a method as abstract. Any subclass must implement (override) this method. Enforces a contract on child classes.
A Python module (from abc import ABC) used to create abstract classes. Child classes must inherit from ABC and implement all abstract methods.
A class inheriting from multiple parent classes. Syntax: class Child(Parent1, Parent2):. Allows combining features from multiple sources.
Checks if an object is an instance of a class, including parent classes. Example: isinstance(dog, Animal) returns True if dog is an Animal or subclass.
A class that inherits from a parent class. Also called derived class or subclass. Syntax: class Dog(Animal):
A class that is inherited by child classes. Also called base class or superclass. Defines shared attributes and methods for child classes.
